result:
I’d make it impossible to dehumanize other people.

Not “harder,” not “socially frowned upon”—actually impossible at a psychological level. You could still disagree, compete, even fight over ideas or resources, but you wouldn’t be able to see others as less real, less worthy, or disposable.

A lot of the worst things in the world don’t start from pure malice—they start from distance. From turning people into abstractions: “them,” “those people,” “collateral,” “users,” “enemies.” Once that switch flips, almost anything becomes justifiable.

If that switch didn’t exist, we’d still have conflict, but it would look very different. Policies would have to account for human impact in a visceral way. Systems couldn’t quietly grind people down. Even selfishness would have limits, because you’d feel what you’re doing to others as something real, not theoretical.

It wouldn’t solve everything. But it would quietly dismantle a huge percentage of what makes the world cruel.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
